subtopics.title: Components
subtopics.content:
- **132 tiles** in 6 colors (22 each)
- **Factory displays** (5-9 depending on player count)
- **1** supply board with tower
- **Player boards** (star-shaped pattern)
- **Scoring track**
- **Wild color reference tiles**
- **1** starting player marker

subtopics.title: Board Setup
subtopics.content:
1. **Place supply board** in center with tower.
2. **Fill bag** with all 132 tiles.
3. **Place factory displays** (2P: 5, 3P: 7, 4P: 9).
4. **Draw 4 tiles** onto each factory display.
5. **Determine wild color** — Flip the round marker; the shown color is wild for round 1.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
- **Player board** — Star-shaped with 6 points, each assigned a color.
- **Score marker** on 0.
- **No starting tiles.**

subtopics.title: Game Overview
subtopics.content: Azul: Summer Pavilion is a tile-drafting game. Draft colored tiles from factory displays and place them on your star-shaped player board. Score by completing stars, surrounding statues, and collecting bonus tiles. Plays over 6 rounds.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
Each round has 3 phases:

#### 1. Acquire Tiles
- Take turns drafting tiles from factory displays or the center.
- Pick all tiles of ONE non-wild color from a factory. Remaining tiles go to center.
- OR pick all tiles of one non-wild color from the center.
- You may also take wild tiles along with your chosen color (max 1-4 depending on spaces needed).
- Place drafted tiles on your personal supply board.

#### 2. Place Tiles
- Take turns placing tiles from your supply onto your star board.
- Each star point requires specific colors and quantities.
- Wild tiles can fill any color requirement.

#### 3. Prepare Next Round
- Determine new wild color.
- Refill factory displays.
- Discard excess tiles from supply (keep max 4).

subtopics.title: Key Actions
subtopics.content:
#### Star Board
- 6 star points, each with spaces numbered 1-6.
- Each space has a specific required color.
- Placing a tile costs tiles equal to the space's number (e.g., the "4" space costs 4 tiles of that color/wild mix).
- **Surrounding bonuses** — Covering all spaces around a pillar, statue, or window scores bonus tiles.

#### Wild Tiles
- Each round, one color is designated wild.
- Wild tiles can substitute for any color when placing.
- You may take wild tiles along with your drafted color.

subtopics.title: Special Rules
subtopics.content:
- **First player** — Whoever takes from the center first gets the starting player tile.
- **Overflow penalty** — Excess tiles beyond 4 at round end are discarded (no penalty, but wasted).
- **Bonus tiles** — Completing surroundings lets you draw tiles from supply and immediately place them if valid.

subtopics.title: Scoring and Endgame
subtopics.content:
#### During Game
- Score points for each tile placed (equal to connected group size).
- Bonus points for surrounding pillars (1-4 pts), statues (2-8 pts), windows (3-12 pts).

#### End Game (After 6 Rounds)
- **Complete star** — Bonus points per fully completed star point.
- **Number set** — Points for having all 6 numbers (1 through 6) filled across all stars.
- **Highest score wins.**

subtopics.title: Beginner Tips
subtopics.content:
- **Use wild tiles efficiently** — Wild tiles are flexible; use them to fill expensive spaces.
- **Plan for surrounding bonuses** — Completing areas around statues and windows yields chain reactions.
- **Don't over-draft** — You can only keep 4 tiles between rounds.

subtopics.title: Key Strategies
subtopics.content:
- **Chain bonuses** — Surrounding bonuses give extra tiles that can trigger further placements.
- **Wild color awareness** — Draft heavily when your needed color is wild.
- **Complete stars** — End-game bonuses for completed stars are substantial.
- **Deny opponents** — Watch what colors opponents need and draft accordingly.
